role_summary: Civil rights movement organizer; in this corpus's embedding his testimony clusters with Jennifer Lawson, Charles McLaurin, Juadine Henderson, Steven McNichols, and Annie Pearl Avery.
ai_reading: Mahone's top semantic neighbors are Jennifer Lawson (entry 59, count 71), Charles McLaurin (entry 17, count 62), Juadine Henderson (entry 68), Steven McNichols (entry 118), and Annie Pearl Avery (entry 3). The cluster crosses Mississippi voter-registration figures (McLaurin, Henderson) with broader SNCC organizers (Lawson, Avery, McNichols); the embedding reads him as an interior-of-the-SNCC-network voice without strong pull toward any single subgroup. His axis positions are all in moderate ranges (-0.14 to +0.40), placing him in the catalog's centrist-voice band alongside McDew, Terry, Richardson, and Hayling, the structural pattern of catalog entries the embedding finds difficult to pull strongly toward any single pole.
biographical_paragraph: Sam Mahone's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 113 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
